title: crotalaria mandrarensis
intro: Crotalaria mandrarensis, a plant species belonging to the legume family, is native to the Mandrare River delta in southern Madagascar. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, providing health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and antimicrobial activity. In traditional herbal preparations, Crotalaria mandrarensis is often used in infusions, decoctions, or as a topical paste to harness its medicinal properties.
